## prompt-2-draft-passage

*Generated by Process A from the submitted `prompts/prompt-enactment.md`, adapted
from the prior build's `adoption/sub-prompts/prompt-2-draft-framework.md` and the
table-of-contents and back-matter conventions of `adoption/final-framework`. This
build is analogous to, and does not rewrite, the prior adoption framework.*

Write the draft-framework (scaffold) LaTeX into
`law-physical-ai-trials/enactment/draft-framework/`, building on the existing
template appearance carried by `adoption/final-framework/publication`
(`frameworkstyle.sty`) but recolored to the new palette. Preserve the visual
structure exactly: the title rule, the abstract box, Linux Libertine body, and the
`L`/`C`/`Y` table column types; change only the accent to `#4B0082` and the figure
palette to `#4B0082`, `#000080`, `#C0C0C0`, black, and grays. The draft is a
scaffold: it lays out the full manuscript structure and, in every body section,
provides bracketed `[DRAFTING INSTRUCTIONS: ...]` that name the exact repository
files and directories the later stages must process, rather than finished prose.

**Structure (one `sections/*.tex` file per section, each `\input` by `main.tex`).**
Front matter (title page on the `#4B0082` theme, abstract, keywords, disclaimer); an
Introduction that defines the lay terms a legislator needs (Physical AI, robot
patient interaction code, VVUQ, verification before generation, the ten gates,
ACCEPT / ESCALATE / BLOCK, section 515D, authorization versus appropriation, PAYGO
and CutGo, regular order) and states the three passage questions ("Would I defend
this vote to a constituent who lost someone to a preventable error?", "Can I explain
the score to the Budget Committee?", "Is there daylight between my party and the
other on this?"); a clickable, page-filling `\tableofcontents`; the **eight body
sections** mapped to the passage questions, in order:

1. `q1-mandate.tex` - Mandate (is the problem real enough to need a Federal statute;
   the embodiment problem; the sequencing gap; the financial-data gap).
2. `q2-authority.tex` - Authority (is this within Congress's power and consistent
   with existing law; the FD&C Act; the rule of construction; the State savings
   clause; the closest existing analogues).
3. `q3-safety.tex` - Safety (what in the bill protects patients; the ten gates; the
   three hard catastrophe predicates; verification before generation).
4. `q4-fiscal.tex` - Fiscal (what it costs and whether it is paid for; the
   authorization schedule; outlays; zero net mandatory spending; the 19 to 1 cost
   reduction; the user-fee carve-out).
5. `q5-constituents.tex` - Constituents (who it helps back home; trial access in
   every State; rural reach; the platform's national rollout; jobs and workforce).
6. `q6-bipartisanship.tex` - Bipartisanship (can one caucus support it without
   losing the other; the innovation appeal and the safety appeal; the deregulatory
   posture priced against the safety floor).
7. `q7-coalition.tex` - Coalition (who is behind it and is opposition manageable;
   medical societies; patient advocates; industry; standards bodies; the absence of
   an organized opponent).
8. `q8-passage.tex` - Passage path (can it move through both chambers and then work;
   committee referral; markup; floor strategy; the Senate companion; the platform
   as proof of implementability; the rulemaking deadline).

Then back matter adapted from `adoption/final-framework`: References,
Acknowledgments, Ethical Disclosures, Rights and Permissions, Cite This Article, and
Data Availability, each added to the table of contents with `\phantomsection`.

**Bracketed drafting instructions must name exact sources**, for example: the bill
text in `single-prompt-bill/auto-bill-02/final-bill/publication` (sections
`s2-findings`, `s3-amendment`, `s4-comparative`, `s5-financial`, and appendices
`a6-cost-estimate`, `a7-verification-economics`, `a8-financial-standard`,
`a9-research-matrix`); the platform scope in
`physical-ai-oncology-trials/national-platform/new_paper/final_paper` (sections
`executive_summary`, `national_mcp`, `psl_usl_standards`, `financial_analysis`,
`regulatory_landscape`, `implementation_strategy`); the bill and prior-work lineage
in `enactment/references/author_works.bib` (entry `Kawchak2026HR9510Bill`,
DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20619762; the platform, the surgical-humanoid VVUQ, and the
patient-journey works); and the legislative and standards references in
`enactment/references/passage_refs.bib`. Each body section names which Mermaid
figure slot and which table it will carry in the full stage.

**Figures and tables.** Define the colored TikZ Mermaid primitive in the style file
(the `psgfig` environment and the themed node styles in the strict palette) and
place at least one labeled figure float or table per section, with captions, even if
the draft renders a labeled placeholder. **Set `\vspace{-0.75cm}` between every
figure and its caption.** Tables use `>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}`
columns summing to `\textwidth`, with column widths chosen for the amount of text
each column carries.

One commit each for `main.tex`, the `.sty` style file, `references.bib`, and
`README.md`; one commit per `sections/*.tex` file. Also commit
`prompt-draft-passage.md` (this prompt verbatim under a `## prompt-draft-passage`
heading) and `output-draft-passage.md` (the framework output). For the
second-to-last commit fix all errors across all files; for the last commit write the
directory README and the `draft-framework-LaTeX.zip` Overleaf bundle and refresh the
pull request.

**Formatting (centralize in the `.sty`).** White background; RaggedRight body with
even interword spacing and no right-margin overflow; widow and club penalties at
10000 so no single line is stranded and no one or two word last line is left behind;
correct "SS" into "§"; single hyphens only (no en, em, double, or triple dashes);
`\clearpage`, `\vspace`, and `\hspace` used as a senior author would to balance
pages. The scaffold must compile in Overleaf with pdfLaTeX. Commit and push in real
time; do not hold commits. Do not stall, ask questions, or go into plan mode.
